channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Hidden Dangers of Scrolling Social Media
item.description: Researchers at Durham University have discovered a surprising side effect of scrolling social media: increased sweating and heart rate. Explore the findings and implications.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Tesla shares plummet as Wall Street rethinks sales projections
item.description: Tesla Inc. shares are on track for their steepest drop since 2020 as Wall Street analysts begin to slash their estimates for vehicle deliveries.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: TikTok Sale: Reid Raisner's $50 Billion Offer Among Four Bids - NewsNation
item.description: President Donald Trump is considering four groups to purchase TikTok, including a $50 billion offer from Wyoming entrepreneur Reid Raisner.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Is the moon too far for your data? IBM's Red Hat is teaming up with Axiom Space to send a data center into space
item.description: IBM’s Red Hat has teamed up with Axiom Space to send a data center to the International Space Station this spring.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: They were forced to scam others worldwide. Now thousands are detained on the Myanmar border
item.description: Thousands of sick, exhausted and terrified young men and women, from countries all over the world squat in rows, packed shoulder to shoulder, surgical masks covering their mouths and, some, their eyes.
item.dc:creator: Huizhong Wu, Jintamas Saksornchai And Martha Mendoza, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Microsoft reportedly struggling to build its own reasoning models to rival OpenAI
item.description: Microsoft may be “all in” with AI, but its efforts to produce homegrown products to feed its ambitions have so far been lagging and its reliance on OpenAI prevails. However, recent events may augur change.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Out of the lab and into the streets, researchers and doctors rally for science against Trump cuts
item.description: Researchers, doctors, their patients and supporters are venturing out of labs, hospitals and offices across the country to stand up to what they call an attack on life-saving science by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Private lunar lander is declared dead after landing sideways in a crater near the moon's south pole
item.description: A private lunar lander is no longer working after landing sideways in a crater near the moon's south pole.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Empowering women in tech: Lessons from a DWP Digital leader
item.description: I’m Head of User Support Services within DWP Digital. In simple terms, if your IT isn’t working, my team is your first protocol to fix it. I have about 450 colleagues in my function, and we support over 90,000 colleagues. I’m also Head of our IT service management profession.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Social Security Administration bans reading 'general news' at work
item.description: The Social Security Administration (SSA) has banned reading “general news” websites at work, The Hill has learned.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: UK watchdog drops competition review of Microsoft's OpenAI deal
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain's competition watchdog said Wednesday it has dropped its review of Microsoft's partnership with OpenAI, saying it's satisfied that the deal doesn't need a closer investigation under the country's merger rules.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: AI pioneers who channeled 'hedonistic' machines win computer science's top prize
item.description: Teaching machines in the way that animal trainers mold the behavior of dogs or horses has been an important method for developing artificial intelligence and one that was recognized Wednesday with the top computer science award.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The best Java and JVM language frameworks
item.description: Not long ago in this space, we compared the 10 best front-end JavaScript frameworks. Now we turn our piercing gaze to the ample supply of back-end frameworks, starting with frameworks built for Java and JVM languages.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Top data storage certifications to sharpen your skills
item.description: Enterprise data storage skills are in demand, and that means storage certifications can be more valuable to organizations looking for people with those qualifications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian joins bid to acquire TikTok
item.description: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian has joined billionaire Frank McCourt’s bid to acquire TikTok as a strategic adviser.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Georgia lawmakers push to ban automated speed cameras near schools
item.description: DECATUR, Ga. (AP) — Outside Beacon Hill Middle School in the Atlanta suburb of Decatur, like along hundreds of roadsides across Georgia, the unblinking eye of a camera tickets drivers who speed through a school zone.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota considers blocking 'nudify' apps that use AI to make explicit images without consent
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Molly Kelley was stunned to discover in June that someone she knew had used widely available “nudification” technology to create highly realistic and sexually explicit videos and images of her, using family photos that were posted on social media.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: AirPods dying? Professor discovers microenvironment that may be responsible
item.description: UT-Austin associate professor Yijin Lu said a similar microenvironment exists in all sorts of electronics. This includes places like your phone and electric vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Eric Henrikson, NewsNation

item.title: Netskope expands SASE footprint, bolsters AI and automation
item.description: Netskope is expanding its global presence by adding multiple regions to its NewEdge carrier-grade infrastructure, which now includes more than 75 locations to ensure processing remains close to end users.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Tariffs for now won’t impact IT organizations
item.description: The impact of tariffs by the Trump Administration on semiconductors from Taiwan is so far not cause for alarm, according to one analyst.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Casey Anthony, mom acquitted in toddler's death, joins TikTok
item.description: Casey Anthony, a Florida mother who was acquitted of murder charges in the death of her 2-year-old daughter Caylee, has joined TikTok to brand herself as a "legal advocate.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Its most recent mega-acquisition is its $28 billion purchase of Splunk, a bold move that adds revenue opportunities in observability, data analytics, AIOps, and genAI.
item.dc:creator: Network World
